programmes.show_description: This time the Goggleboxers watch The Apprentice, Britain's Got Talent, Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing, Love Is Blind, Celebrity Puzzling, and Alexander Armstrong in India.
...Experiment: Curious couples sex-periment with opening up their relationships. Connor and Kirstie explore having a threesome. Chanice and Richardo face an unexpected issue.
Down City: Gordon Ramsay tries to save struggling restaurant Down City in Providence, Rhode Island. But tough-talking owner Abby refuses to take advice from anyone.
Comedians Jamali Maddix and Vittorio Angelone and former Conservative politician Penny Mordaunt dissect the week's news with Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker.
Comedian Adam Buxton and Joe Lycett peruse Prague's wonders, as they kayak down the River Vltava, sample sausages out of skulls and caviar-laced ice cream, and enjoy a beer spa.
In Glasgow, there's an impromptu sing-song, a kitchen disaster and an MC battle, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
Keith Brymer Jones and Marj Hogarth continue their quest to turn a 163-year-old chapel in Pwllheli into their dream home. It's time to rip out the chapel's rot and fungus...
Kirstie and Phil help two sets of house-hunters who've relocated to Aberdeen for work and need to find new family homes fast.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Colin Jackson in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Rebecca's Lover... Not: Rebecca's first boyfriend visits Boston, but she's unaware of the changes in his life. Sam starts a support group for people with stolen sports cars.
Bar Wars VI: This Time It's for Real: The new owner of Gary's Old Town Tavern reacts badly to the gang's latest prank, but is he really the dangerous character he seems?
Heeeeeere's Cliffy!: Norm tricks Cliff into believing that one of his jokes is going to be used on The Tonight Show with Johnny Carson. Sam and Woody install a satellite dish.
Dame Sarah Storey is at the wheelchair curling to watch GB's round robin match against favourites China - who've won gold at the past two Winter Paralympics - in the Mixed Team event.
GB's clash against reigning Paralympic champions China in the Mixed Team wheelchair curling draws to a close. Plus: finals in various classifications of 10k cross-country skiing.
Day 5 continues with cross-country skiing finals, before attention turns to the gold and bronze medal matches in the wheelchair curling Mixed Doubles event.
Channel 4's midday news summary.
Continued coverage of the gold and bronze medal matches in the Mixed Doubles wheelchair curling.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Colin Jackson in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: Our enterprising expats open for business in France and Spain, but power cuts strike Brian and Paula's restaurant and illness hits Jen and Nick's horse charity.
The new run continues. Brothers John and Andrew host the week's third visit at the glampsite on their family farm in Dumfries and Galloway, where things get heated with saunas and hot tubs.
The new run continues. In Manchester, Amy throws an American-themed dinner party. But after one guest gets a mouthful of tin foil, will grandma's secret rub smooth things over? (Ep3/5)
Ade Adepitan's in Tesero to watch some cross-country skiing action. And there are highlights from the wheelchair curling, where it's medal match day in the Mixed Doubles event.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
The Green Party: Mothin Ali, Deputy Leader of the Green Party, sets out the party's plans for more investment in community projects.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: In Alnwick, Kirstie and Phil meet ministers Chris and Caroline, who need a divine intervention. Caroline loves their home and neighbours, but Chris craves more space.
Epic rags-to-riches drama, based on Barbara Taylor Bradford's bestseller, charting Emma Harte's rise from maid to mogul. With Brenda Blethyn, Jessica Reynolds and Emmett J. Scanlan. (Ep1/8)
PC Jodie and PC Zoe are called to break up a brawl between neighbours. And veteran officer Emma struggles to identify a child who's found wandering the streets late at night.
A dangerous driver tries to flee the scene of a crash. An altercation between housemates needs some police attention. And Pete's copper's nose uncovers something unexpected.
The Coniston Mountain Team help a paraglider who's crash-landed on Great How Crag. And a man's gone missing around the tunnels of a disused quarry at Hodge Close. (Ep3/4)
Grasshopper Also: In Carlstadt, New Jersey, Gordon Ramsay attempts to save the Grasshopper Also, a family-owned Irish restaurant that's on its last legs.
(2014) Thriller with Sameena Jabeen Ahmed. A woman runs away with her boyfriend. But her traditionalist family track her down.
Keith and Marj turn the old schoolroom into a snug, complete with a baroque chair and feature wall, before discovering Capel Salem's fiery past at the National Library of Wales.
In south London, Kirstie and Phil help Jo and Ally hunt for a family home. And Jennifer is looking for the purr-fect period property with outside space for her cats.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: On the Isle of Wight, restorer Ronnie relives his past life in Ibiza via a three-wheeled trike.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Colin Jackson in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
An Old-Fashioned Wedding (Part 1/2): Kelly and Woody fight the desire to consummate their marriage before the ceremony. Carla predicts the big day will be marred by disaster.
An Old-Fashioned Wedding (Part 2/2): Woody and Kelly put on a brave face as their big day is hit by disasters involving the guests, the priest, the kitchen staff and the cake.
The Little Match Girl: Rebecca accidentally sets fire to the bar with a discarded cigarette end. Plagued by guilt, she lies to Sam, telling him that faulty wiring is to blame.
Can ParalympicsGB's Menna Fitzpatrick add yet more weight to her groaning trophy cabinet in the Women's Para Alpine Skiing Giant Slalom? Teenage debutante Hester Poole is also in the mix.
Run 1 of the Women's Para Alpine Giant Slalom continues. As GB's most decorated winter athlete, Menna Fitzpatrick is a good bet for the podium. But will a recent knee injury hamper her?
The Women's Para Alpine Skiing Giant Slalom reaches its climax. Then it's off to catch up on all the drama from the final round robin matches in the Mixed Team Wheelchair Curling.
Channel 4's midday news summary.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole visit Witney in Oxfordshire, where Henry discovers a tractor that hasn't run for years - but it's no problem for restorer Alan, the mechanical medic.
Debra and Gordon search for a holiday home in Torrevieja for £120k. Debra's keen on apartments, but Gordon wants a townhouse. Can Laura Hamilton find a compromise?
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Colin Jackson in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: Donna fears that she can't deliver the luxury stays she's promised in Italy. Paul and Melissa face challenges restoring a once-prestigious French hotel.
The new run continues. Sue hosts the week's final visit, at her Victorian townhouse B&B in Penrith, where a marmalade tasting is followed by some bittersweet discussion of payment day.
The new run continues. Can Vanessa ruffle feathers with burlesque bravado, a piña colada crumble and face yoga on the penultimate night in Manchester? (Ep4/5)
Can ParalympicsGB's Menna Fitzpatrick medal in the Women's Para Alpine Giant Slalom? GB's Mixed Curling team take on Norway in their last round robin match before the semis.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
The Labour Party: Natalie Fleet, MP for Bolsover, sets out the Government's vision for tackling the cost-of-living crisis.
George meets people taking on builds motivated by strong emotional ties. Chris, who has stage four cancer, and Ellie return to Chris's Ribble Valley roots to build a dream home.
Epic rags-to-riches drama with Brenda Blethyn, Jessica Reynolds and Emmett J. Scanlan. Emma and Edwin's forbidden love deepens, but can they ever overcome their class differences? (Ep2/8)
Scott's rushed into Queen's Medical Centre by air ambulance after crashing his motorbike at 100mph. And Peter has been crushed by an eight-tonne static caravan.
